title: Temporal Goods.
content:
These temporal goods God, the most wise, commends
    To th' good and bad in common for two ends:
    First, that these goods none here may o'er-esteem
    Because the wicked do partake of them;
    Next, that these ills none cowardly may shun,
    Being, oft here, the just man's portion.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Temporal Goods.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
